programmes.show_description: Bubble Trouble: As the team relaunches their investigation on Skinwalker Ranch, they are surrounded by a shocking phenomenon. (S6, ep 1)
Thousands of UFO sightings and abductions are reported every year. This series examines these accounts and casts new light on historic cases. (S1, ep 9)
Werewolves From Space: Bulletproof wolves attack a family farm, an April Fool's prank awakens a real-life horror and a driver picks up an unwanted and very hairy passenger. (S1, ep 2)
Final Showdown: The swamp faces a crisis like never before with the alligator population now at record levels. The threat has never been bigger, and the stakes never higher. (S11, ep 13)
Earth Shaker: Troy and Pickle face a familiar foe, while Bill and Tes defend a friend's camp from a slithering invasion. Zak and Aaron's hunt leaves a bad taste in their mouths. (S3, ep 5)
American Jewellery & Zoo: Les makes a deal to get the kids off his back, but when some interesting animals come through the door, it might be too much for him to resist. (S9, ep 8)
Join antiques expert and presenter David Dickinson as more collectors try to sell their items to dealers. Will they strike a deal or take a chance at auction instead? (S9, ep 29)
Caernarfon 1: David Dickinson and his team of antique dealers are in Caernarfon in North Wales, where a 90-year-old lady pushes Karen Dalmeny to her limits. (S10, ep 9)
The guys visit New Brunswick, where they uncover vintage automobiles, a retro coffin and a collector's bargain-priced inventory. (S4, ep 7)
Henry Cole, Allen Millyard and Guy Willison visit Phil and Annika's maze-like sheds, where they find a pair of 1940s motorbikes. (S3, ep 3)
Does a Bear Bid in the Woods?: Kenny heads out to the mountains, keeping an eye out for cougars, while Lisa scores on a small sister locker. (S15, ep 18)
California Knows How to Pottery: Ivy kidnaps Pops and takes him to Rosamond where they score a locker, while Kenny sets out to brighten everyone's day. (S15, ep 19)
Homefront Heat: Ashley is livid when she gets cut out of a deal by Les and Seth and the battle spills onto the sales floor in a huge way. (S9, ep 11)
Strike Out: Sibling rivalry is at an all-time high between Seth and Ashley, and when she scores two baseball deals that are clearly outside her comfort zone, Seth cries foul. (S9, ep 12)
No Sleep Til Vegas: A seller sends in the original cover art for one of the greatest albums of all time, and Rick fights for his right to buy it.
Graduation Day: Brock and Aaron have to fill their tags, while King of the Swamp Troy Landry hunts with his dad and Willie gives his son a final exam. Season finale. (S11, ep 14)
The Snake Pit: Troy and Pickle receive a tip about a snake pit. Meanwhile, Bill and Tes investigate a quarry full of pythons and Dusty has a close call with the supernatural. (S3, ep 6)
The guys visit New Brunswick, where they uncover vintage automobiles, a retro coffin and a collector's bargain-priced inventory. (S4, ep 7)
If Ya Got It, Flaut It: Kenny happens upon a familiar vehicle and follows it to Lancaster for an auction, while Rene tries to find new luck after losing in Vegas. (S15, ep 20)
Buyers head to storage unit auctions across California. They are only allowed a brief of glimpse of what's inside before deciding how much they are prepared to bid. (S6, ep 1)
Henry Cole, Allen Millyard and Guy Willison are back! This time, they revamp their stall after picking up a vanload of trinkets. (S3, ep 4)
Cosmic Impacts: Meteor impacts have created the perfect environment for humankind. Could new evidence reveal that celestial objects have been sent by an alien intelligence? (S15, ep 6)
There is no safe place. Every day, all around the world, in the most ordinary places, regular people are spotting UFOs. (S1, ep 1)
The Pawn Strikes Back: Rick gets a shot at an original Eastman Kodak camera, while Corey has a sweet tooth for an unopened box of 1980s Star Wars candy.
Does a Bear Bid in the Woods?: Kenny heads out to the mountains, keeping an eye out for cougars, while Lisa scores on a small sister locker. (S15, ep 18)
California Knows How to Pottery: Ivy kidnaps Pops and takes him to Rosamond where they score a locker, while Kenny sets out to brighten everyone's day. (S15, ep 19)
Caernarfon 2: Caernarfon in North Wales is the destination for David Dickinson and his team today, where a lady gives Michael Hogben a hard time for being stingy. (S10, ep 10)
Unidentified Flying Monsters: Old legends warn of flying cryptids who descend from the sky but new witnesses connect this aerial aggression to something more alien in nature. (S1, ep 4)
Bucks County: In this groundbreaking series, our episodes feature an array of opinions from a very select group of specialists. (S1, ep 9)
Discover never-before-seen truths about UFOs through revelations from those on the inside. Is the truth about another intelligence becoming undeniably clear?
Thousands of UFO sightings and abductions are reported every year. This series examines these accounts and casts new light on historic cases. (S1, ep 10)
Bloodsuckers and Flying Saucers: Something is killing the animals. Unexplained livestock deaths in Puerto Rico and aliens abduct a dog in England. (S1, ep 3)
Graduation Day: Brock and Aaron have to fill their tags, while King of the Swamp Troy Landry hunts with his dad and Willie gives his son a final exam. Season finale. (S11, ep 14)
The Snake Pit: Troy and Pickle receive a tip about a snake pit. Meanwhile, Bill and Tes investigate a quarry full of pythons and Dusty has a close call with the supernatural. (S3, ep 6)
You Bet Your Butt: Seth and Ashley have betting on the brain and start the day with a wager that leaves one of them dressed to the nines. (S9, ep 10)
Join antiques expert and presenter David Dickinson as more collectors try to sell their items to dealers. Will they strike a deal or take a chance at auction instead? (S9, ep 30)
Summer of Saucers: In 1947, there was an unprecedented wave of UFO sightings that propelled the UFO phenomenon to the world stage. What were the US Air Force's findings? (S4, ep 13)
Police Sightings: For many people, the most credible UFO reports are those raised by law enforcement. We investigate the most significant reports from police records. (S4, ep 14)
Space Shuttle Encounters: Between 1981 and 2011, cameras on NASA space shuttles have picked up many images of UFOs that experts still struggle to explain.
Mass Sightings: While many UFO sightings occur in rural locations with only a single eyewitness, there have been sightings over cities witnessed by hundreds. (S4, ep 16)
Experts open previously off-limits files in a bid to answer some of the most mysterious questions surrounding UFOs and extra-terrestrial life. (S1, ep 6)
Experts open previously off-limits files in a bid to answer some of the most mysterious questions surrounding UFOs and extra-terrestrial life. (S1, ep 7)
An out-of-this-world look at ten of the most fascinating alien encounters ever. (S1, ep 8)
The Top Ten Extraordinary Discoveries: Ten archaeological finds, including mysterious spheres and a lost city, challenge our views on human origins and extraterrestrial links. (S17, ep 1)
Yakutat: In this groundbreaking series, our episodes feature an array of opinions from a very select group of specialists. (S1, ep 5)
Tinley Park Lights: In this groundbreaking series, our episodes feature an array of opinions from a very select group of specialists. (S1, ep 8)
Alien Pilots, Carolina Lights and Stone Cold Animals: Did a man in Turkey capture an Alien piloting a UFO? What are the strange lights above Brown Mountain in North Carolina? (S3, ep 14)
Mystery Sea Beast, the Earth is Alive and Shapeshifting UFO: What is the mysterious sea beast seen from UK shores? Are summoners able to commune with UFOs? Is the Earth alive? (S3, ep 15)
Terror of the Ice Snake: Discover a trail of moving objects in the ice of Antarctica, evidence of one of Joseph Stalin's most insane schemes and a code in the Sonoran Desert. (S6, ep 6)
Curse of Genghis Khan: A strange structure discovered in the Gobi Desert could reveal the whereabouts of lost treasure - changing everything we know about Genghis Khan. (S6, ep 7)
The King at Roswell: When a strange plane is discovered near the sight of the Roswell UFO incident, experts wonder if the aircraft has Nazi or extraterrestrial origins. (S6, ep 8)
Decoding the Mysterious Antikythera Mechanism: The discovery of an ancient device leads historians to theorise about the origins of the Antikythera mechanism. (S4, ep 8)
Blackbeard's Lost Treasure: After centuries of searching, Blackbeard's epic hoard remains missing. As new evidence emerges, the mystery endures - what became of the treasure? (S4, ep 9)
The Puzzling Pyramids of Egypt: Delve into the theories that could help explain why and how the ancient Egyptians came to build the pyramids. (S4, ep 10)
Aliens From the Deep: A medieval monk battles a massive serpent and the legend of the Loch Ness Monster is born. (S1, ep 5)
U.S.S. Nimitz: In this groundbreaking series, our episodes feature an array of opinions from a very select group of specialists. (S1, ep 10)

programmes.show_name: Alien Contact: The Pascagoula UFO Encounter
programmes.show_description: A seminal alien abduction is re-opened as new eye witness accounts confirm the horrifying UFO encounter. Hear from lone survivor Calvin Parker with shocking new details.
